summary: Based on the presentation of neck stiffness, bilateral shoulder tenderness, elevated inflammatory markers (ESR and CRP), and a family history of ischemic stroke, systemic lupus erytematous (SLE) is strongly suspected. Screening for heliotrope rash—a characteristic finding in SLE—is appropriate since its presence would confirm diagnosis and address underlying immune-mediated causes contributing to both joint symptoms and increased stroke risk.

Answer choice: B
